content: Self-Refine’s iterative output polishing and Reflexion’s dynamic memory-backed self-reflection can be combined to create a hierarchical autonomous improvement framework: Self-Refine handles low-level output refinement for individual tasks (like code debugging or writing drafting) while Reflexion accumulates high-level, cross-task reflection on common failure patterns, turning single-task iterative feedback into cumulative long-term capability growth for LLMs. This framework retains both methods’ core advantage of requiring no extra supervised training.
